title: cyclic-difference-set-781-300-115
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize a conditional mod-11 slice, not seal a paper-ready packet. The blocking gap is mathematical closure after the invalid w=71, t=5 multiplier step, not formalization labor.
micro_paper_assessment: Fails the bounded micro-paper audit. The surviving claim is stronger than an example, but only as a conditional mod-11 structural slice; once the invalid w=71 route is removed, the remaining gap is not genuinely small.
family_affinity: strong cyclic residual-row case; multiplier-orbit contraction remains the natural lane
generalization_signal: The mod-11 orbit compression may transfer, but the current run does not justify the mod-71 reduction needed for an exact-instance theorem.
strongest_honest_claim: Under the valid mod-11 multiplier premise from Baumert-Gordon Theorem 3.2, any hypothetical cyclic (781,300,115) difference set has mod-11 contraction equal to (15,28,29) or (30,23,31), up to swapping the two nonzero 5-orbits; in particular, the index-11 subgroup contains either 15 or 30 points.
paper_title_hint: No current publication title is recommended; at most: 'Mod-11 Contraction Constraints for a Hypothetical Cyclic (781,300,115) Difference Set.'
candidate_theorem_slice: Under the valid mod-11 multiplier premise from Baumert-Gordon Theorem 3.2, any hypothetical cyclic (781,300,115) difference set has mod-11 contraction equal to (15,28,29) or (30,23,31), up to swapping the two nonzero 5-orbits.
next_action: Preserve the mod-11 slice, discard the w=71, t=5 route as theorem evidence, and move the candidate aside until a new exact obstruction or construction route closes the C_781 row without the invalid multiplier step.
